name: Route export: MP4 download
description: Expose an endpoint that returns a single MP4 file for a given route + camera (front/driver/wide). Internally concatenates the HLS segments already produced by the transcoder (we don't re-encode). Uses FFmpeg's concat demuxer or '-c copy' mux from ts to mp4. Streams the result so we don't buffer the whole mp4 in memory or on disk.
acceptance_criteria: GET /v1/routes/:dongle_id/:route_name/export.mp4?camera=f|e|d (default f) streams an MP4 built from the route's HLS .ts segments in order

Implementation uses ffmpeg via exec.Command; stdin list of concat entries, stdout piped to the HTTP response; Content-Type: video/mp4; Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="<route_name>-<camera>.mp4"
Returns 404 if the route has no HLS segments for the requested camera; 400 for an invalid camera code
The request is cancellable: if the client disconnects, the ffmpeg process is killed (use exec.CommandContext with request context)

internal/api/export_test.go covers: happy path (asserts 'ftyp' box appears in first 64 bytes of the response), 404 on missing HLS, 400 on bad camera, cancellation kills ffmpeg (use a short-lived context and verify process is reaped)
go vet ./... and go build ./... pass cleanly
